visual_checks: Look for session timeout behavior by checking if the page redirects to a login screen after a period of inactivity
Check if there are any warnings before a session timeout occurs
After re-authenticating following a timeout, verify whether previously entered data (form fields, shopping cart, draft content) is preserved
Look for save or auto-save mechanisms that persist user data before session expiration
Check if multi-step forms or workflows retain progress after re-authentication
Verify that the user is returned to the same point in their workflow after logging back in
pass_conditions: User data entered before session timeout is preserved and restored after re-authentication
Form data, shopping cart contents, and draft content survive session expiration and re-login
Multi-step processes retain the user's progress after re-authentication
The user is returned to the same point in their activity after re-authenticating
Auto-save or data persistence mechanisms ensure no user data is lost during session timeout
fail_conditions: User data entered before session timeout is lost after re-authentication
Form fields are cleared and must be re-entered after logging back in following a timeout
Shopping cart contents or selections are lost when the session expires
Multi-step processes restart from the beginning after re-authentication instead of resuming where the user left off
Draft content (emails, documents, comments) is discarded upon session expiration
na_conditions: Page does not require authentication or have session timeouts
No user data entry or stateful interactions exist on the page
The application does not use session-based authentication
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Session timeout behavior could not be triggered or observed during evaluation
common_mistakes: Clearing all form data when redirecting to the login page after a timeout
Shopping cart implementations that tie cart contents to the session rather than the user account
Multi-step checkout or application processes that restart from step 1 after re-authentication
Not auto-saving draft content before session expiration
Redirecting the user to the homepage after re-authentication instead of their previous location
Relying solely on client-side storage that may be cleared when the session ends

examples.pass: A user filling out a long insurance application form is timed out and redirected to login; after re-authenticating, all previously entered form data is restored and they continue from where they left off
examples.fail: A user completing a multi-page job application is logged out due to inactivity; after logging back in, all form data is lost and the application must be started over from the first page
examples.partial: After re-authentication, some form fields are preserved but file attachments and selections in dropdown menus are lost and must be re-entered

plain_meaning: When an authenticated session expires, the user must be able to resume what they were doing without losing data after logging back in. The intent is that users who step away or need more time are not punished by losing their work on re-login.
scope_applies_to: Multi-step forms or checkout flows behind authentication
Data-entry workflows that span session boundaries
Any authenticated activity with in-progress state
scope_does_not_apply_to: Content where session loss does not destroy user work
Warnings about timeout duration (that is SC 2.2.6)
borderline_calibration: If the page has no authentication or no data entry, this SC is N/A.
Auto-saving drafts, preserving form state across re-login, or restoring cart contents all satisfy the SC.
Loss of transient UI state (scroll position) is not a failure; loss of typed data is.
